prd.oneLiner: Connect automation requests, triggers, steps, conditions, approvals, execution history, error handling, and operations analysis.
prd.goal: Maintain 95% or more successful execution for recurring-work automation and reduce manual effort.
prd.whyNow: Repeated data entry, approvals, notifications, and handoffs between systems depend on people and chat, creating missed steps, delays, and errors.
prd.problem: Repeated data entry, approvals, notifications, and handoffs between systems depend on people and chat, creating missed steps, delays, and errors.
prd.solution: Connect automation requests, triggers, steps, conditions, approvals, execution history, error handling, and operations analysis.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Business contributor requesting or building repeatable automation
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Repeated data entry, approvals, notifications, and handoffs between systems depend on people and chat, creating missed steps, delays, and errors.

targets.name: Business automation and IT operations coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Repeated data entry, approvals, notifications, and handoffs between systems depend on people and chat, creating missed steps, delays, and errors.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Repeated data entry, approvals, notifications, and handoffs between systems depend on people and chat, creating missed steps, delays, and errors.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Maintain 95% or more successful execution for recurring-work automation and reduce manual effort.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Automation request, trigger, step, and integration-setting registration
Workflow, execution history, error, approval, and integration-status lookup
Automation design, review, approval, execution, error reprocessing, and change handling
Automation run rate, time saved, failure, reprocessing, and ownership reporting
Automation request, trigger, step, and integration-setting registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
